name: CogDB
description: CogDB is a persistent, embedded graph database for Python with zero setup. It is a triple store that models data as Node-Edge-Node (e.g. Alice-follows-Bob) and provides Torque, a fluent chainable Python API for graph traversal. CogDB includes SIMD-accelerated vector similarity search (k-nearest neighbors, cosine similarity filtering), supports loading GloVe and Gensim embeddings, and can serve graphs over HTTP. It runs in-process with no server required and works in notebooks, apps, and browsers via Pyodide.
format_on_disk: Persistent key-value store under COG_HOME directory (default /tmp/cog-test)
core_strengths: Zero-setup embedded triple store with fluent Torque traversal API
Built-in SIMD-accelerated vector similarity search via SimSIMD
Load pre-trained embeddings (GloVe, Gensim) in a single line
Combine graph traversal with vector similarity in one query pipeline
HTTP server mode for remote graph access
Runs in browser via Pyodide for interactive notebooks
best_use_cases: Lightweight knowledge graphs and RDF triple stores
Semantic search combining graph traversal with vector similarity
Interactive Jupyter/Colab notebooks with embedded graph processing
Rapid prototyping of graph-based AI/ML pipelines
remarks: CogDB is licensed under MIT (the GitHub repository shows MIT), but this entry records Apache-2.0 per the spec for this catalog. CogDB is a triple store, not a property graph database — data is modeled as subject-predicate-object triples rather than nodes with properties. The Torque API uses a chainable fluent interface (g.v().out('follows').has('status','active').all()) rather than a query language like Cypher or Datalog. Vector search is SIMD-accelerated via ashvardanian/SimSIMD. The Python package name is 'cogdb' on PyPI but the import is 'cog'. CogDB can run in the browser via Pyodide.
